seoTitle: Bombay vs Bombo — Climate Risk Comparison | ClimateNest
seoDescription: Compare climate risk scores between Bombay and Bombo. See which suburb is safer from flood, bushfire, heat, and storm hazards.
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Bombay and Bombo.
heading: Bombay vs Bombo — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Bombay, New South Wales and Bombo, New South Wales. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Bombay has a lower overall climate risk score (39/100) compared to Bombo (40/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Bombay or Bombo safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Bombay has a flood risk score of 52/100, while Bombo scores 56/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Bombay scores 52/100 for bushfire risk. Bombo scores 15/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Bombay or Bombo?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Bombay has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 1.48% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
